sections.title: Introduction to Gender
sections.content: All French nouns have a grammatical gender - either masculine or feminine. The gender of a noun affects the form of articles, adjectives, and sometimes verbs that agree with it.
examples: le livre (the book) - masculine
la table (the table) - feminine
un garçon (a boy) - masculine
une fille (a girl) - feminine

sections.title: Masculine and Feminine Nouns
sections.content: Most nouns ending in -e are feminine, but there are many exceptions. Many nouns ending in -tion, -sion, -ité are feminine. Many nouns ending in consonants or -age are masculine.
examples: Feminine: la maison, la voiture, la nation, la décision, la rapidité
Masculine: le chat, le livre, le voyage, le fromage

sections.title: Adjective Agreement
sections.content: Adjectives must agree in gender and number with the nouns they describe. Add -e for feminine singular, -s for masculine plural, -es for feminine plural.
examples: un chat noir (a black cat) - masculine singular
une voiture noire (a black car) - feminine singular
des chats noirs (black cats) - masculine plural
des voitures noires (black cars) - feminine plural

sections.title: Regular Adjective Patterns
sections.content: Most adjectives follow regular patterns for agreement. The base form is masculine singular, and endings are added for feminine and plural.
examples: grand: un grand arbre, une grande maison, de grands arbres, de grandes maisons
petit: un petit enfant, une petite fille, de petits enfants, de petites filles
bon: un bon livre, une bonne idée, de bons livres, de bonnes idées

sections.title: Irregular Adjectives
sections.content: Some adjectives have irregular feminine forms that must be memorized.
examples: blanc → blanche (white)
sec → sèche (dry)
doux → douce (soft)
fou → folle (crazy)
long → longue (long)
gros → grosse (fat)

sections.title: Position of Adjectives
sections.content: Most adjectives come after the noun (BAGS: Beauty, Age, Goodness, Size often come before). Most descriptive adjectives come after.
examples: une belle voiture (a beautiful car) - before
une voiture rouge (a red car) - after
un grand homme (a tall man) - before
un homme intelligent (an intelligent man) - after

sections.title: Practice Exercise
sections.content: Write 15 sentences using adjectives. For each sentence, use a different adjective and make sure it agrees correctly in gender and number with the noun.
